Professional Experience

Progressive Casualty Insurance – Senior Data Analyst

2019 - Current

Job objectives

  • Guided leadership in the effective allocation of staffing for claims handling.
  • Used latest techniques and modern technologies to efficiently obtain, transform, model, and explain patterns in staffing and claims volume data.
  • Communicated findings through several media, including HTML presentations, Tableau dashboards, and Excel spreadsheets.
  • Automated several recurring reports and ETL procedures on linux servers.
  • Convinced leadership to organize and begin using git for critical code.

Contributions external to role

  • As the foremost proponent of R at Progressive, I:
    • Developed several packages including tools to speed SQL connections, extend ggplot with a company color palette, and other miscellaneous functions.
    • Spent 100 hours in 2021 providing one-on-one support to analysts all over the company
    • Helped facilitate R Beginner’s Workshops, and bi-weekly R Clinics.
    • Presented at company R meetups.
    • Contributed to maintenance of a universal setup and configuration utility.
  • Won 2nd place in company-wide modeling challenge, involving over 15 teams.

Cornell University – Graduate Research Assistant

2015 - 2018

  • Developed two novel statistical models to estimate animal population size, applied to moose in New York.
  • Tested statistical model performance via simulation analysis using high-performance computing techniques using an original dual-layer parallelization scheme of a cluster of computers.
  • Gained competency with Bayesian inference using Markov Chain Monte Carlo simulation, geostatistical modeling, maximum likelihood optimization procedures, and generalized linear models.
  • Applied motivational and effective leadership leading field research teams of up to 10 personnel into wilderness conditions, and leading laboratory discussions on the use of Git and R Markdown to improve organization of research.
  • Communicated results to statistical and ecological audiences nationally and internationally.

Education

  • Cornell University, Ithaca, NY
    • M.S. Quantitative Ecology. 2018. GPA: 4.0

  • University of Nebraska, Lincoln, NE
